conditionsHeading.title: What's behind your knee pain?
conditionsHeading.lead: Knee pain rarely has one cause. These are the conditions we most commonly help patients manage with the right support.

conditions.title: Osteoarthritis (OA)
conditions.description: Cartilage wear that causes pain, stiffness, and reduced mobility â€” especially common after age 50. Offloader bracing can shift load away from the affected compartment.

conditions.title: ACL / Ligament Injury
conditions.description: Sports and accident-related ligament tears that need stability and protection during rehabilitation and return-to-activity.

conditions.title: Post-Surgical Recovery
conditions.description: After total knee replacement, ACL reconstruction, or arthroscopy â€” controlled motion and cold therapy speed safe recovery.

conditions.title: Patellofemoral Pain
conditions.description: Pain around or behind the kneecap, often during activity. Patellar tracking braces can help relieve pressure.

conditions.title: Meniscus Injury
conditions.description: Cartilage tears causing swelling, locking, or pain. Functional bracing supports the knee while it heals.

conditions.title: General Instability
conditions.description: If your knee 'gives way,' even on flat ground, you may benefit from a stability brace tailored to your activity level.

seoContent.title: Understanding knee pain â€” and what helps
seoContent.intro: Knee pain is one of the most common reasons Indian adults visit an orthopaedic specialist. The right support, used at the right time, can meaningfully reduce pain and help you stay active â€” but it isn't a substitute for medical advice. Here's what to know.

sections.heading: Why does my knee hurt?
sections.body: The knee is a complex joint that takes load every time you walk, sit, or climb stairs. Pain can come from worn cartilage (osteoarthritis), inflamed soft tissue (tendinitis or bursitis), ligament injury, or wear from repeated activity. The cause matters â€” because the right support depends on it.

sections.heading: How does bracing actually help?
sections.body: Modern functional bracing does three things: it offloads the painful part of the joint, controls excess motion that worsens pain or risks re-injury, and provides proprioceptive feedback so your muscles support the joint better. For osteoarthritis specifically, an offloader brace can reduce pain enough to delay or avoid surgery in many patients.

sections.heading: What about cold therapy and compression?
sections.body: For acute injuries and post-surgical recovery, controlling pain and swelling early is critical. Cold therapy with active compression â€” like the Polar Care Wave system â€” has been shown to reduce post-operative pain medication needs and speed return-to-function compared with ice packs alone.

sections.heading: When should I see a doctor?
sections.body: If your knee pain lasts more than two weeks, involves significant swelling, your knee gives way under you, or you can't fully bend or straighten it â€” see an orthopaedic specialist. Bracing supports treatment; it does not replace a diagnosis.

sections.heading: How do I choose the right brace?
sections.body:
• Match the brace to your condition â€” an OA brace is different from a post-surgical brace.
  • Get the right size; an ill-fitting brace can do more harm than good.
  • Speak to your treating doctor or our trained specialists before purchase.
  • Wear it correctly and consistently â€” bracing only works if it's used.

seoContent.disclaimer: This page is educational. It is not a substitute for medical advice. Please consult a qualified orthopaedic specialist before starting any bracing or treatment programme.

faqHeading.title: Frequently asked questions about knee pain

faq.question: Will a knee brace cure my osteoarthritis?
faq.answer: No. Osteoarthritis is a progressive condition and there is no brace, supplement, or device that reverses cartilage loss. What a well-fitted offloader brace can do is meaningfully reduce pain, improve walking comfort, and help many patients delay or avoid surgery. Used together with physiotherapy, weight management, and your doctor's guidance, bracing is one of the most evidence-supported non-surgical options available.

faq.question: How long should I wear my knee brace each day?
faq.answer: It depends on the brace type and your condition. Offloader OA braces are typically worn during weight-bearing activities â€” walking, working, light exercise â€” and removed when you rest. Post-surgical braces follow your surgeon's protocol and may be worn most of the day in early recovery. Always follow the wearing schedule your treating doctor recommends.

faq.question: Can I wear a knee brace under my clothes?
faq.answer: Yes. Most modern braces are designed with a low profile and can be worn comfortably under loose trousers. Our team can help you choose a brace suited to your daily routine.

faq.question: Are these products available across India?
faq.answer: Yes. All products featured here are sold and shipped pan-India by our authorised retail partner Osteokart.com, with delivery typically within 3â€“5 business days. Avana's clinical team supports fitting and orientation across major Indian cities.

faq.question: Do I need a doctor's prescription to buy a brace?
faq.answer: Most knee bracing products do not legally require a prescription, but we strongly recommend you consult an orthopaedic specialist before purchasing. The wrong brace, or the wrong size, can be uncomfortable at best and harmful at worst.

faq.question: What if the brace doesn't fit or doesn't help?
faq.answer: Speak to our specialist team. Sizing issues are usually resolved with a refit. If a brace genuinely isn't right for your condition, we will help you find one that is â€” or refer you back to your treating physician for a different approach.
